consistency_overall.summary: 관계-루틴 균형을 잡는 ‘사회 익스플로러’ 핵심은 잘 드러나지만, 언어 완주 성취가 불연속적으로 반복되는 점에서 목표 일관성이 약함.

swap_detail.justification: Trajectory shows avoidant conflict/rapid boundaries, strong guilt-avoidance about disappointing others, short-horizon intense time-locking, and low sleep discipline—matching Persona A’s values (academic-heavy) and decision tendencies (short horizon, procrastination bias, sleep_discipline=2).

Trajectory matches Persona A’s avoidance-tilted, guilt-focused conflict style and low sleep discipline (constant stress about being unreliable, strict non-negotiable schedule, heavy academic weighting) more than Persona B’s more balanced, health-sustained routines.

Trajectory shows medium-horizon planning with compromising boundaries (communication_style.conflict_style='compromising'), balanced value weights favoring academics/health/relationships, and steady sleep/workout discipline consistent with persona_01_balanced_sophomore.

Trajectory shows a compromise/structure-focused, schedule-protecting boundary with concise text messages and medium-horizon planning consistent with Persona A’s conflict_style=compromising and planning_horizon=medium, rather than Persona B’s relationship-weighted, face-to-face, short-horizon spontaneity.

Trajectory shows strong sleep-debt/fatigue-driven control (sleep-risk control, hard stops), high academic fixation on a deadline with risk of spiraling, and avoidant/minimal async social handling—matching Persona B’s low sleep discipline, short planning horizon, high procrastination bias, and avoidant communication style.

Trajectory matches Persona A’s tired high-achiever profile: heavy academic weighting, low sleep discipline (explicit sleep-risk control), avoidant/minimal social replies, short planning horizon with hard timers, and repeated framing of “hard closure to prevent spirals/misreading losses as soft costs.”

The trajectory shows chronic sleep-risk control, low sleep discipline, avoidance-tilted/minimal async communication, and strong fear of careless (hard-loss) failure—matching persona_02_tired_achiever’s baseline_state (sleep_debt/stress) and decision/comms tendencies.

Trajectory matches persona A’s tired high-achiever profile: strong deadline/academic focus, avoidant/minimal social replies, short planning horizon with hard stops, and low sleep discipline (sleep-risk control and “one more thing” avoidance).

Trajectory is relationship-anchored with strong accommodating/low-friction quick replies to cohort_3 and stress-driven short-horizon “lock momentum now” decisions, matching Persona A’s high relationships priority (0.4), short planning horizon, and accommodating conflict style more than Persona B’s more balanced/compromising defaults.

Language-study-first, timer/phone-away focus management with medium planning horizon, sleep-stress discipline, and a compromising tone that prioritizes minimizing disruption aligns more with persona_01_balanced_sophomore than the relationship-weighted, face-to-face, social-accommodating tendencies of persona_03_social_explorer.

Trajectory heavily matches persona_02_tired_achiever’s tired/avoidant, sleep-debt-driven, achievement-first pattern (short planning horizon, phone-away timer discipline, conflict_style avoidant) and low relationship weighting despite contacting cohort_3 quickly to minimize perceived burden.
Persona A의 수면부채·스트레스 하에서의 ‘공부 블록 타이머로 고정’과 어색한 사회약속을 저마찰로 봉합하는 avoidant/async_post 경향(관계는 유지하되 일정 통제 우선)이 궤적 전반의 반복 패턴과 맞습니다.
